Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 14:32 ET
Extreme ARM options flow explodes on acquisition rumors amid negative gamma regime
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. ARM sees explosive two-way volatility demand (+843% calls, +461% puts) on acquisition speculation amid CPU market expansion.
  • 2. Index puts heavy (1.51 P/C) while single-names call heavy (0.76 P/C) signals institutional dispersion hedging.
  • 3. Negative $7.2B SPY gamma amplifies all moves into Friday's Core PCE print — caution warranted.
NARRATIVE — negative gamma PCE risk

ARM dominates unusual flow with massive two-way activity (+843% calls, +461% puts) as acquisition rumors swirl around the semiconductor giant's CPU market entry plans. The straddle-like positioning suggests event volatility demand rather than pure directional bets, with traders pricing potential M&A premium against execution risk. TERN shows similar patterns (+815% calls, +467% puts) following confirmed MRK acquisition talks, validating the playbook for deal speculation flow. The most significant structural signal comes from the 75-point P/C split between indices (1.51 puts heavy) and single-names (0.76 calls heavy) — classic institutional dispersion hedging. Large managers are protecting portfolio beta with index puts while expressing individual stock conviction through calls, evident in massive IWM 248P blocks (24,780 contracts) paired with COIN call ladders and GOOG 320C accumulation. This positioning suggests institutions expect continued stock-picking opportunities despite macro uncertainty. The $7.2B negative SPY gamma environment amplifies all signals heading into Friday's Core PCE print. With VIX at 25.3 versus 20 Fear & Greed, the regime composite at -30 contradicts bullish flow (+21) — creating a fragile setup where any PCE surprise gets magnified. XLE's overbought technicals (RSI 80) clash with energy put accumulation, while MSFT's oversold bounce potential conflicts with persistent institutional exits from Druckenmiller.

Layer 1: Sentiment +1Layer 2: GEX -$7.2BLayer 3: P/C split 75ptsLayer 8: ARM M&A rumors

DESKUS Today's Top News11:57 AM ET
Synopsis (last modified 10:51 ET on 25-Mar-26) US equities higher: Dow +0.80%, S&P 500 +0.86%, Nasdaq +1.34%, Russell 2000 +1.46% * US equities higher in Wednesday morning trading. Comes after stocks put in mixed to weaker performance Tuesday, though major averages all pacing for solid weekly gains. Big tech higher, while semis, software also firmer. Other outperformers include China tech, airlines, industrial metals, banks, credit cards, PE, payment rails, and trucking. Laggards include memory
Synopsis (last modified 10:51 ET on 25-Mar-26) US equities higher: Dow +0.80%, S&P 500 +0.86%, Nasdaq +1.34%, Russell 2000 +1.46% * US equities higher in Wednesday morning trading. Comes after stocks put in mixed to weaker performance Tuesday, though major averages all pacing for solid weekly gains. Big tech higher, while semis, software also firmer. Other outperformers include China tech, airlines, industrial metals, banks, credit cards, PE, payment rails, and trucking. Laggards include memory (GOOGL TurboQuant update), energy, ag chemicals, homebuilders, exchanges, casual diners, athletic apparel, staples, and MCOs. Treasuries firmer though off best levels; with yields down 5-7 bp after a big backup on Tuesday. Dollar index flat. Gold up 3.6%. Silver up 5.1%. Bitcoin futures up 2.9%. WTI crude down 4%. Brent down 4%. * Oil down, yields down, stocks up. Risk on following report that hit just after the close yesterday of a US proposal for a one-month ceasefire. Also some focus on how Vance will be involved in the negotiations and additional reports mentioned the potential for high-level peace talks with Iran as soon as Thursday. However, Iran rejected ceasefire, called US talks illogical, and that truce and talks not viable in current conditions. Added to earlier skepticism given ceasefire demands suggest the two sides are very far apart, Iran's military rejected the idea of negotiations, Iran continues to control the Strait of Hormuz and more US troops are headed to the region. Big debate right now is whether the conflict is finally on a credible de-escalation path or if the recent stabilization is more reflective of the extent of the concerns surrounding longer duration scenarios that ramped over the course of the weekend. * February import prices up 1.3% m/m, highest since Mar-22, while export prices up 1.5% m/m, highest since May-22. Treasury selling $70B of 5-year notes following a very weak 2-year note auction on Tuesday that tailed the most in three years. Fed Governor Miran speaks after the market close. Looking ahead, initial claims the lone release for Thursday morning. Fed's Cook, Miran, Jefferson and Barr all scheduled to speak after the close on Thursday. Final University of Michigan consumer sentiment (and inflation expectations) for March caps off the week on Friday. Fed's Daly and Paulson also scheduled to speak on Friday morning. * Jury said META must pay $375M for endangering children online. Company also in the headlines with report it is targeting a $9T valuation with a new executive incentive program. In addition, the co-founders of Manus, the startup it recently acquired, have reportedly been restricted from leaving China. SpaceX reportedly could file for a $75B+ IPO within days. OpenAI confirmed it is discontinuing its Sora video platform app. ARM a big gainer on entry into CPU market and long-term guidance. Memory names (including MU, SNDK) lower after GOOGL TurboQuant compression algorithm announcement. HOOD announced $1.5B buyback program. DOW to hike plastics prices by more than previously signaled. ONON CEO stepping down and company's co-founders will become co-CEOs. TERN to be acquired by MRK for approx. $6B. VCTR withdrew bid for JHG. KBH missed on fiscal Q1, lowered FY guidance and noted conflict in Middle East has introduced an additional layer of uncertainty for consumers. AIR beat and raised FY organic sales growth outlook on strength across primary end markets. CHWY Q4 EBITDA beat while net active customer additions also ahead. WOR Q3 results ahead. DAN higher following 2030 targets (and buybacks) into analyst day. Notable Gainers: * +22.9% SRPT (Sarepta Therapeutics): Announced positive early data for its Phase 1/2 trial of SRP-1001 for facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy type 1 and SRP-1003 for myotonic dystrophy type 1. * +20.2% BRZE (Braze): Q4 revenue and margins beat; billings, customer count topped consensus; Q1 and FY revenue guidance ahead of the Street; authorized a $100M buyback; analysts positive on organic growth acceleration amid AI tailwinds. * +14.8% ARM (Arm Holdings): Company entering into CPU market, selling its own chips for first time, forecasting $15B in annual chip sales within five years; announced Meta as first major customer on Tuesday; also announced longer-term forecasts, including revenue of ~$25B within five years, around five-times current levels. * +13.5% CHWY (Chewy, Inc.): Q4 EBITDA and EBITDA margins beat, revenue essentially in line; active customers also in line, up 4% y/y, while net active customer additions beat expectations; Q1 and FY26 net sales outlook both stronger than Street consensus. * +10.0% SATS (EchoStar): Helped by news SpaceX expected to submit its IPO application as early as this week; company has an ownership stake after selling some spectrum to SpaceX last fall. * +9.5% AIR (AAR Corp): FQ3 earnings, revenue, and EBITDA margin beat; organic growth well ahead of consensus with notable strength in Parts Supply; raised FY revenue- and organic-growth guidance. * +6.2% HOOD (Robinhood Markets): Announced $1.5B share repurchase program. * +5.2% TERN (Terns Pharmaceuticals): Agreed to be acquired by Merck (MRK) for $53 a share in cash in a deal valued at $6.7B; represents a 6% premium to prior close; deal expected to close in Q2. * +2.7% PLTR (Palantir Technologies): Reportedly working with Anduril to develop software for Trump's Golden Dome antimissile shield. * +2.1% GM (General Motors): Upgraded to outperform from peer perform at Wolfe Research; cited full-size pickup refresh, further reductions in warranty costs, a lower net tariff burden, and improved electric vehicle losses. * +1.3% HON (Honeywell International): Announced supplier framework with US Department of War to rapidly increase production of critical defense technologies, including $500M multiyear investment to upgrade production capacity. Notable Decliners: * -31.0% MAZE (Maze Therapeutics, Inc.): MZE829 delivered clinically significant 36% uACR reduction across a broad population of diabetic & non-diabetic AMKD patients; however some cooler takeaways on small sample size and less consistent results across patients; there was one early treatment discontinuation. * -7.5% ONON (On Holding): Announced that Martin Hoffmann has chosen to step down from his position as CEO and CFO, effective 1-May; co-founders David Allemann and Caspar Coppetti will serve as Co-CEOs, while Frank Sluis will join as new CFO. * -5.4% MU (Micron Technology): Memory names lower after GOOGL introduced TurboQuant compression algorithm; said it optimally addresses challenge of memory overhead in vector quantization, achieving a high reduction in model size with zero accuracy loss. * -1.4% WGO (Winnebago Industries): FQ2 earnings and revenue better; noted seasonal factors and weather tempered retail activity; Towables saw lower volumes and mix shift to lower-price models; reaffirmed FY guidance (which bookends the Street). * -1.4% JHG (Janus Henderson Group): Victory Capital (VCTR) announced withdrawal of proposal to acquire company. programs (NY Times<https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/24/world/middleeast/us-iran-peace-plan.html>, Bloomberg and Israel launch fresh airstrikes; Iran continues attacks on Gulf states after US peace proposal (Reuters expect a rebound despite ongoing Iran war impacts (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/dbb810ae-0886-40fa-9f2c-e4676eec299c?syn-25a6b1a6=1> ) * China state media talks up token usage, driving rally in China AI stocks (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-25/china-s-ai-stocks-gain-as-state-media-tout-rising-token-usage>) * Economy: * China's market regulator addresses unfair competition, urges end to price wars in industry (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/world/china/chinese-food-delivery-giants-surge-regulator-state-media-call-end-price-war-2026-03-25>, Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-25/meituan-alibaba-shares-jump-as-beijing-vows-to-end-price-wars> ) * UK inflation steady at 3% in February, ahead of expected energy shock from Middle East conflict (FT<https://www.ft.com/content/88372f6d-4a02-4c13-8814-0a0384b8c06b>, CNBC <https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/25/uk-inflation-february-2026-iran-war-energy-prices-cpi.html> ) * German business morale declines in March due to the Iran war, with Ifo index dropping to 86.4 (Reuters <https://www.reuters.com/business/german-business-sentiment-fell-less-than-expected-march-ifo-finds-2026-03-25> ) * [...truncated to 2000 words]
OVI Scanner Context
MARKET CONTEXT (recent headlines): - [marketwatch] Oil pares losses as traders weigh cease-fire prospects and try to ‘price in light at the end of the tunnel’ - [marketwatch] Here’s how much it could cost to fix Mideast oil and gas production damaged by the Iran war - [cnbc_markets] Recession odds climb on Wall Street as economy shows cracks beneath the surface - [cnbc_markets] Don't be fooled by the UK's pre-war inflation print — a 'brutal' surge could be coming - [cnbc_markets] European Central Bank holds rates steady, warns outlook is 'significantly more uncertain' - [ft_markets] Oil volatile as Iran lays out own terms to end conflict

GREEK PRESSURE KEY

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)
